diagrams.title: Optimizer Comparison: Update Formulas
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of weight update formulas for Adagrad, RMSprop, and Adam optimizers, highlighting key differences in how each handles gradient accumulation and learning rate adaptation.
elements: Adagrad update equation: θ = θ - η/√(G + ε) * g
RMSprop update equation: θ = θ - η/√(E[g²] + ε) * g
Adam update equation: θ = θ - η/(√(V̂) + ε) * M̂
diagrams.purpose: Show the mathematical differences in how each optimizer computes parameter updates, demonstrating Adagrad's accumulating squared gradients, RMSprop's exponential moving average, and Adam's combination of first and second moment estimates

diagrams.title: Adam Optimizer Components Breakdown
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how Adam combines two mechanisms: momentum (first moment) for direction persistence and RMSprop-style adaptive learning rates (second moment) for gradient magnitude scaling.
elements: Bias correction blocks for M and V
diagrams.purpose: Explain the dual mechanism of Adam optimizer - how it maintains both a velocity term (like momentum) and adapts learning rates per parameter (like RMSprop), making it effective for sparse gradients and non-stationary objectives

diagrams.title: Optimizer Trajectories on Loss Surface
diagrams.description: Visual representation of different optimization paths on a loss surface contour plot, showing how Adagrad, RMSprop, and Adam navigate toward the minimum compared to basic gradient descent.
elements: Adagrad path (rapid initial progress, diminishing learning)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the practical difference in convergence behavior - showing how adaptive learning rate methods (Adagrad, RMSprop, Adam) handle ill-conditioned loss surfaces better than vanilla gradient descent
